content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN nodes rooted at node 111. Each node iii has an associated color CiC_iCi​ and a value ViV_iVi​.

For any tree with **at least two** nodes, the **score** for a partition (obtained by removing a **single leaf** — that is, a node with degree 111) is defined as the sum, over all colors, of the absolute differences between the total values of nodes with that color in the two resulting components. For tree consisting of **single** node, its **score** is defined as the value of the single node.

More formally,
Given a tree TTT with **at least two** nodes, colors CiC_iCi​ and values ViV_iVi​, you are allowed to remove **exactly one leaf node** from the tree — that is, a node with degree 111.
Removing this leaf node uuu partitions the tree into two connected components:


AAA: the remaining tree after removing uuu,
BBB: the singleton component consisting of the removed leaf.
The **score** of this removal is defined as:
Score(A,B)=∑c∈C∣ ∑v∈ACv=cVv  −  ∑w∈BCw=cVw∣
\mathrm{Score}(A,B)
= \sum\limits_{c \in \mathcal{C}}
  \left|\,
    \sum\limits_{\substack{v \in A \\ C_v = c}} V_v
    \;-\;
    \sum\limits_{\substack{w \in B \\ C_w = c}} V_w
  \right|
Score(A,B)=c∈C∑​​v∈ACv​=c​∑​Vv​−w∈BCw​=c​∑​Vw​​If tree TTT consists of single node uuu, its **score** is defined as the value of the single node, that is VuV_uVu​.

Your task is to compute the **maximum score** for the subtree of each node **independently**. That is, for each node i, obtain the maximum score considering **only** the subtree rooted at i.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — the number of nodes of the tree.
The next N−1N-1N−1 lines describe the edges. The iii-th of these N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting an edge between uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​.
The next line of each test case contains NNN space separated integers — the iii-th integer denotes the color of node iii, that is CiC_iCi​.
The next line of each test case contains NNN space separated integers — the iii-th integer denotes the value of node iii, that is ViV_iVi​.




Output Format
For each test case, output NNN integers each on a new line, the iii-th integer denotes the maximum partition score that can be achieved for subtree of node iii.
constraints: The given edges form a tree.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the first test case:


For subtree of node 333, it is optimal to remove 666 as leaf from the subtree.

Contribution to score by color 111 is ∣1+5∣=6|1+5| = 6∣1+5∣=6
Contribution to score by color 222 is ∣5+7−4∣=8|5+7-4| = 8∣5+7−4∣=8
Contribution to score by color 333 is ∣2∣=2|2| = 2∣2∣=2



For subtree of node 555, it is optimal to remove 555 as leaf from the subtree.


For subtree of node 666, which consists of single node, its score is its value, that is 444.
